hero.headline: Democracy is daily, not just on election day.
hero.sub: CivicOS keeps the public record of citizen action between elections — issues reported, petitions signed, responses received — on your street, in your community, today.
hero.trust: Open source · Verified accounts · Audit-trailed in UTC

parties.title: Built for everyone in public life

citizens.title: Make your voice count, every day
citizens.body: Report issues on your street, sign petitions for what you care about, follow your representatives, and see what gets done — in days, not election cycles.

reps.title: Show your work in public
reps.body: Respond to your constituents in one place. Your response rate sits on your public profile — accountability is the design, not a feature.

government.title: A trusted channel to the public
government.body: Route citizen reports to the right office, publish initiatives, and demonstrate progress with a verifiable, timestamped record.

ngos.title: Mobilise around real evidence
ngos.body: Aggregate community signals, organise petitions, and measure impact with structured civic data instead of anecdote.

articles.title: Continuous participation, not just at election time

issues.title: Report and track issues
issues.body: Pothole, streetlight, unsafe road — file it in three minutes with photos and follow the status from open to under review to resolved.

petitions.title: Start and sign petitions
petitions.body: Goal-based petitions with automatic milestones at 25%, 50%, and 100%. One citizen, one signature, no inflated numbers.

reps.title: Follow your representatives
reps.body: See who represents you, what they have said, and how quickly they respond — all on a public profile that updates itself.

discover.title: Discover what matters nearby
discover.body: A feed ranked by proximity: your community first, then your LGA, then your state — so local always reads first.

notifications.title: Stay in the loop in real time
notifications.body: Notifications stream live the moment an issue updates, a petition crosses a threshold, or your representative replies.

transparency.title: Transparency by default
transparency.body: Every interaction is timestamped and traceable. Public information stays public; personal data stays private.

principles.title: Five principles, written before the code
principles.note: Just as digital banking modernised how we move money, CivicOS modernises how we participate in democracy — transparent, accessible, and built to last.

steps.title: From sign-up to civic action in under five minutes

join.title: Join the register
join.body: Sign up with your name and email. Identity-verified, no paperwork, no fees.

pick.title: Pick your patch
pick.body: Select your state and LGA so we know what counts as "near you" in your feed.

act.title: Take action
act.body: Report an issue, sign a petition, follow your representatives — your name, your record.

follow.title: See what gets done
follow.body: Real-time updates as status changes, responses arrive, and milestones land.

cost.a: Yes. CivicOS is free for citizens — forever. Reporting issues, signing petitions, following your representatives, and receiving updates cost nothing. Public institutions and NGOs may have paid tiers for advanced tooling, but the citizen experience is free.

privacy.a: Your email, phone number, and address are private and never shown publicly. Only your display name, role, and the public actions you take (issues you raise, petitions you sign, comments you post) are visible to other users. You control what you share.

reps.q: Do representatives actually use the platform?
reps.a: Representatives are onboarded community by community. Their response rate is visible on their public profile — accountability is built into the design, so silence is also a signal.

coverage.q: What if no one represents my area yet?
coverage.a: You can still join, pick your community, and file. Once a representative for your constituency joins, your existing activity links to them automatically.

ownership.q: Who built CivicOS — and who owns it?
ownership.a: CivicOS is open civic infrastructure, built in the open and stewarded by an independent team. The codebase is public, the architecture is documented, and contributions are welcome. No political party, single funder, or commercial interest controls the platform.

abuse.q: How do you prevent abuse, spam, or misinformation?
abuse.a: Every account is identity-verified at sign-up. Comments and submissions are rate-limited, moderated against community guidelines, and traceable to a real account. Petitions require unique signatures — one citizen, one signature.

newsletter.title: Subscribe to The Dispatch — be first to know when CivicOS lands in your area
newsletter.sub: One short email per month with product updates, community milestones, and rollout news. No marketing fluff. Unsubscribe anytime.
newsletter.doneSub: We'll be in touch as we expand coverage.

cta.title: Ready to take part?
cta.sub: Join citizens, representatives, and organisations building a more accountable civic life — together, on the record.

footer.mission: Strengthening democratic participation through secure, transparent, accessible digital infrastructure.

login.title: Enter your civic command center.
login.description: Track community issues, sign petitions, and keep your representatives accountable from one place.
login.cardSubtitle: Use your CivicOS credentials to continue.
login.error: Could not sign you in. Check your email and password and try again.

register.title: Build your citizen profile.
register.description: Join your local community space, report issues faster, and shape public decisions between elections.
register.cardSubtitle: It takes less than a minute to get started.
register.error: Could not create your account. Try a different email or try again shortly.

onboarding.title: Let's get you set up.
onboarding.description: Pick your community so your feed, notifications, and representatives all point to your patch. You can change this later from your profile.
onboarding.stateHint: Choose the state you want to be active in on CivicOS.
onboarding.communityHint: These are the CivicOS communities set up in {{lga}}, {{state}}. Choose the one closest to where you live or care about most.
onboarding.communityEmptySub: Nobody has created a CivicOS community for {{lga}}, {{state}} yet. You can still browse the platform — the yellow banner on Discover will remind you to come back when one appears.

forgot.title: Forgot your password?
forgot.description: Enter the email you signed up with. We'll send a link to set a new password.
forgot.cardSubtitle: The link is good for one hour.
forgot.sentSub: If an account exists for {{email}}, a reset link is on its way. Give it a minute — check spam too.
forgot.unknownEmailNote: For your security we don't confirm whether an email is registered.

reset.title: Set a new password.
reset.description: Pick something you haven't used elsewhere. Minimum 8 characters.
reset.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
reset.successSub: You're signed in. Time to get back to what matters.
reset.expiredSub: Reset links last 1 hour. Request a new one to continue.
reset.invalidTitle: This reset link is no longer valid.
reset.invalidSub: It may have already been used. Request a new one if you still need to reset.

verify.sentSub: We sent a verification link to {{email}}. Click it to confirm your account — the link is good for 24 hours.
verify.sentTip: Don't see it? Check spam, or use the button below to resend.
verify.successSub: You're all set. You can now file issues, sign petitions, and join the conversation.
verify.invalidTitle: This verification link is no longer valid.
verify.expiredSub: Verification links last 24 hours. Sign in and request a new one from your profile.
verify.invalidSub: It may have already been used. Sign in and request a new one if your email isn't verified yet.
verify.bannerTitle: Verify your email to unlock everything.
verify.bannerSub: We sent a link to {{email}}. Until you click it, you can browse but not file, sign, or comment.
